# Paperclip Hub

> The plugin directory for Paperclip — an agent orchestration platform at https://paperclip.ing.

## What this is

Paperclip Hub (cliphub.fyi) lists community-published plugins for Paperclip. It is *not* a product of its own — there is no API, no auth, no docs to read. It is a static directory that resolves each plugin's manifest from npm and serves the result.

For Paperclip itself:
- Docs: https://docs.paperclip.ing
- Discord: https://discord.gg/m4HZY7xNG3
- GitHub: https://github.com/paperclipai/paperclip

## Discovering plugins

The full registry is published as JSON at:

  https://cliphub.fyi/plugins.json

Each entry has: slug, npmPackage, addedBy, sourceRepo, version, manifest (id, displayName, description, capabilities, …), installs (weekly npm downloads), and resolvedAt timestamps. Prefer fetching this file over scraping HTML.

Categories: auth, provider, tools, integration, observability, memory, other.

## Installing a plugin

Each plugin is an npm package. Install via the Paperclip CLI:

  npx paperclipai@latest plugin install <package>

## Submitting a plugin

Plugin authors submit a small "pointer" file (just npm package name + GitHub handle + category) via https://cliphub.fyi/submit, which pre-fills a GitHub pull request. CI fetches the plugin's npm tarball, validates ownership against npm maintainers, and statically extracts the manifest. There is no review queue.

## Source

The hub itself lives at https://github.com/lacymorrow/paperclip-hub.
